<!--
Thanks for contributing! Please fill out the sections below.
Keep PRs focused on a single concern where possible.
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR change, and why? -->

## Type of change

<!-- Put an "x" in all that apply. -->

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature / enhancement
- [ ] Content change (theory data, questions, blends, or copy)
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Tooling / CI / chore

## Verification

<!-- All three should pass before requesting review. CI runs them too. -->

- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes
- [ ] `pnpm build` succeeds

## Content & sourcing checklist

<!--
Complete this section only if your PR touches theological content
(theory data, questions, blends, or user-facing copy). Delete it otherwise.
-->

- [ ] Theories are presented neutrally; strengths and critiques stay attributed
      to proponents and critics rather than asserted in the project's own voice.
- [ ] Attributions (figures, traditions, dates) are accurate and not overstated.
- [ ] Any new source links resolve to real, relevant, reputable pages and were
      checked to load.
- [ ] No sources, quotations, or attributions were fabricated.
- [ ] If question count, scoring, or blends changed, tests and the counts in
      `README.md` were updated to match.

## Notes for reviewers

<!-- Anything else worth calling out: tradeoffs, follow-ups, screenshots, etc. -->
